newsSince: Materially positive new catalyst: on June 16 Circe Energy ordered ~2 GW of Cummins natural-gas generation (HSK78/QSK60 gensets) for a West Texas AI data-center campus and future North American deployments, deliveries 2026-2030. Cummins also raised 2026 revenue guidance to +8% to +11% (EBITDA margin 17-18%), lifted its 2030 revenue target to $45-50B, and announced a new 4 MW natural-gas engine purpose-built for data-center power. This concretely strengthens the AI-power thesis; +6% move since June 9.

sources.label: Cummins +9.3% on 2 GW Circe Energy AI data-center deal (Simply Wall St, June 19)
Cummins natural gas generators to power West Texas data centers (Cummins IR)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha vs passives: a concrete 2 GW AI data-center gas-gen order (Circe), raised 2026 and 2030 guidance, and a dedicated 4 MW data-center engine pull Cummins meaningfully closer to the power-bottleneck core this page rewards - more than 'Monitor/benchmark' implies. Genuine fundamental step-up warrants a move up.
verdicts.bull: 2 GW AI campus order + new 4 MW DC engine + raised 2026/2030 guidance make onsite-power exposure concrete and durable.
verdicts.neutral: Real, growing AI-power demand but still a diversified industrial, not a pure bottleneck pure-play.
verdicts.bear: $98B cap, +36% YTD already prices much of the AI-power optionality; cyclical truck/engine core.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 2 GW Circe order + 4 MW data-center natural-gas engine ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: Diversified-industrial dilution; AI-power optionality partly priced
